.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:97:- What did not work: there was no new implementation work to inspect, so most value came from dependency synthesis rather than novel discovery.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:98:- What I would do differently: for iteration 32, I would convert each Q1/Q2/Q3 milestone into packet-ready success criteria and explicit verification gates so the roadmap can be executed without re-arguing sequencing.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:100:## Recommended Next Focus

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:144:## Assessment
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:145:- **New information ratio**: `0.23`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:146:- **Questions addressed**: unit-vs-integration boundaries, memory-quality regression strategy, compaction/bootstrapping verification, scope/offline-failure testing, and how much of Mnemosyne to test directly versus as an adapter contract.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:147:- **Questions answered**: wrapper behavior should be unit-tested with mocks; stateful Public behaviors should use real SQLite fixtures; retrieval quality should be gated by ablation/ground-truth metrics; compaction needs payload snapshots; scope/offline matrices should precede any facade rollout.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:148:- **Novelty justification**: This pass adds a concrete three-lane testing architecture tied to current Public sources instead of only comparing feature patterns at the design level.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:152:## Ruled Out

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:159:- **What did not work**: The external repo offers no observable backend internals and minimal packaging metadata, so novel insight came from boundary analysis rather than deeper implementation inspection.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:160:- **What I would do differently**: In iteration 34, I would convert this into explicit CI lanes and named fixture packs with pass/fail thresholds per lane.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:162:## Recommended Next Focus

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:180:- Questions answered: [list]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:185:- New information ratio: `0.24`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:186:- Questions addressed: `[search latency trade-offs, storage growth controls, indexing overhead, startup-time impact, compaction overhead, code-search separation]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:187:- Questions answered: `[Public’s main costs come from ranking depth and startup integrity work; its main savings come from incremental indexing and active-corpus reduction; Mnemosyne’s main advantage is cheap wrapper ergonomics, not lower total runtime cost]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:188:- Novelty justification: `This pass adds a performance-specific synthesis that separates thin plugin overhead from backend cold-start cost and maps Mnemosyne’s cheap compaction/scoping ergonomics onto Public’s heavier but more resilient retrieval lifecycle.`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:191:## Ruled Out
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:196:## Reflection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:198:- What did not work: CocoIndex semantic search was not usable in this turn, so I relied on targeted source reads and exact-term sweeps for the Public-side evidence.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:199:- What I would do differently: next pass should turn these static findings into explicit p50/p95 measurements for `memory_search`, startup scan, and `memory_index_scan` across small, medium, and large fixture corpora.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:201:## Recommended Next Focus

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:4634:- New information ratio: `0.18`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:4635:- Questions addressed: `[compaction-hook compatibility, scope-model conflicts, CocoIndex/code-graph boundary, wrapper-vs-core fit, AGENTS/workflow translation, offline/runtime assumptions]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:4636:- Questions answered: `[Mnemosyne’s best transfer is prompt-level memory discipline and a thin ergonomic facade; its project/global scope model conflicts with governed Public scope; its memory surface should stay separate from CocoIndex and code-graph routing; any adoption should delegate into Public’s existing recovery/save authority rather than create a second backend]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:4637:- Novelty justification: `This pass adds a concrete compatibility map between Mnemosyne’s wrapper-level patterns and Public’s current routing, compaction, scope-governance, and save-authority surfaces rather than only comparing memory features in isolation.`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:4640:## Ruled Out
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:4646:## Reflection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:4648:- What did not work: the external repo proves almost nothing about backend ranking internals, so the highest-confidence findings came from interface and lifecycle analysis, not deeper algorithm inspection.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:4649:- What I would do differently: next pass should turn these conclusions into a concrete adapter matrix with three columns: transport-only prompt injection, MCP facade delegation, and explicit non-goals.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:4651:## Recommended Next Focus

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:3602:- Questions answered: the safest near-term adoption is still the compaction reminder pattern, but only as a deduped add-on to existing compaction transport; AGENTS guidance should be rewritten for governed saves instead of copied; project/global and `core=true` need wrapper-only prototypes because Public’s scope and constitutional semantics are stricter than Mnemosyne’s; a native OpenCode facade is viable only if it delegates to MCP and never becomes a second memory authority. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:3679:- Questions addressed: adoption risk for compaction reminders, startup/AGENTS policy reuse, project/global ergonomic aliases, `core=true` mapping, thin plugin delegation, offline-first failure handling, and separation from CocoIndex/code-graph responsibilities
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:3680:- Questions answered: the safest near-term adoption is still the compaction reminder pattern, but only as a deduped add-on to existing compaction transport; AGENTS guidance should be rewritten for governed saves instead of copied; project/global and `core=true` need wrapper-only prototypes because Public’s scope and constitutional semantics are stricter than Mnemosyne’s; a native OpenCode facade is viable only if it delegates to MCP and never becomes a second memory authority. Validation command run: `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh "/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main" --strict`, which returned `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, while also emitting `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:3682:## Reflection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:3684:- What did not work: the external repo still exposes no backend ranking internals beyond the README contract, and the read-only sandbox prevented updating `research/research.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, `checklist.md`, or saving phase memory.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:3686:## Recommended Next Focus
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:54:## Assessment
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:55:- New information ratio: 0.84
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:56:- Questions addressed: wrapper-visible ranking behavior, query preprocessing, FTS/BM25/vector contract, Public overlap, adaptive fusion depth, scope ergonomics, offline-first cost
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:57:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper proves only a thin search bridge; its hybrid ranking claims are README-level; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval with query routing, lexical weighting, adaptive fusion, and fallback tiers; the most transferable search idea is simpler project/global memory tool ergonomics, not the backend retrieval stack; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:59:## Reflection

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:63:## Recommended Next Focus

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5778:## Assessment
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5779:- New information ratio: 0.90
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5780:- Questions addressed: dedup/upsert behavior, revision tracking, delete semantics, garbage collection, soft deletes, topic key stability, compaction hygiene guidance, auditability of mutation flows
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5781:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper exposes no dedup, no upsert, no revision lineage, no soft-delete lifecycle, and no retention/GC contract; contradiction hygiene is primarily agent guidance plus hard delete; basename-derived project scoping is materially less stable than Public’s canonical-path plus logical-key approach; the compaction reminder block is the only clear hygiene improvement worth borrowing immediately; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5783:## Reflection

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5787:## Recommended Next Focus

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:287:## Reflection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:289:- What did not work: [approach that failed]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:291:## Recommended Next Focus
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:335:4. **HALT:** Stop immediately if uncertain, if line numbers don't match, or if tests fail. (See "Halt Conditions" below).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:339:- **Never lie or fabricate** - use "UNKNOWN" when uncertain.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:341:- **Use explicit uncertainty:** Prefix claims with "I'M UNCERTAIN ABOUT THIS:".
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:439:3. Parse request → Check confidence AND uncertainty (see §4)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:440:4. **Dual-threshold:** confidence ≥ 0.70 AND uncertainty ≤ 0.35 → PROCEED. Either fails → INVESTIGATE (max 3 iterations) → ESCALATE. Simple: <40% ASK | 40-69% CAUTION | ≥70% PASS
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5583:## Assessment
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5584:- New information ratio: 0.78
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5585:- Questions addressed: compaction-survival leverage, governed mutation mismatch, project/global ergonomics, AGENTS/startup reuse, `core=true` semantics, OpenCode plugin layering, offline-first tradeoffs, current hybrid-retrieval overlap, CocoIndex separation.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5586:- Questions answered: Phase A adopt now: add a deduped memory-tool reminder to the existing compaction transport, and rewrite startup/AGENTS wording around `session_bootstrap` plus governed save flows. Phase B prototype later: add read-only `project/global` recall aliases that compile down to current retrieval scope, with no write aliases. Phase C hold or reject: do not add a direct `core=true` shortcut, do not replace Public’s retrieval stack with Mnemosyne, and only pursue an OpenCode-native facade if it delegates to MCP instead of becoming a second authority.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5587:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s genuinely new value for Public is not hybrid ranking, it is wrapper ergonomics plus compaction-time memory awareness.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5591:## Reflection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5593:- What did not work: the external repo is intentionally too thin to prove backend ranking internals, and the sandbox is read-only, so I could not update `research/research.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js`. CocoIndex MCP searches also returned immediate cancellation in this session, so the final ranking relies on direct source tracing rather than a semantic sweep.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5595:## Recommended Next Focus
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5677:## Assessment

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5680:- Questions answered: Phase A adopt now: add a deduped memory-tool reminder to the existing compaction transport, and rewrite startup/AGENTS wording around `session_bootstrap` plus governed save flows. Phase B prototype later: add read-only `project/global` recall aliases that compile down to current retrieval scope, with no write aliases. Phase C hold or reject: do not add a direct `core=true` shortcut, do not replace Public’s retrieval stack with Mnemosyne, and only pursue an OpenCode-native facade if it delegates to MCP instead of becoming a second authority.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5681:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s genuinely new value for Public is not hybrid ranking, it is wrapper ergonomics plus compaction-time memory awareness.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5991:- New information ratio: 0.79
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5992:- Questions addressed: hybrid retrieval boundary, model/runtime implications, project-vs-global scoping, compaction survival, AGENTS guidance, CocoIndex separation, plugin-vs-MCP adoption shape, offline-first tradeoffs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5993:- Questions answered: the wrapper is thin; hybrid ranking is README-level not wrapper-owned; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval; project/global split is useful but collision-prone; compaction tool reminders are the best adopt-now idea; CocoIndex should stay separate from memory retrieval
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5997:## Reflection

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:1587:6154:- Questions answered: the wrapper is thin; hybrid ranking is README-level not wrapper-owned; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval; project/global split is useful but collision-prone; compaction tool reminders are the best adopt-now idea; CocoIndex should stay separate from memory retrieval
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:2417:18991:- Questions addressed: schema visibility, scope keying, canonical row store vs collection store, vector and FTS persistence, lineage and deletion semantics, companion-table breadth, extension-coupled operability
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:2418:18992:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s checked-out repo does not prove backend schema details; Public already runs a canonical multi-index SQLite design; basename-derived collection scoping is weaker than Public’s row-level identity model; Public’s lineage/projection tables are materially richer than Mnemosyne’s visible CRUD contract; any future binary/plugin facade needs explicit health and schema introspection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:2437:19084:- Questions addressed: schema visibility, scope keying, canonical row store vs collection store, vector and FTS persistence, lineage and deletion semantics, companion-table breadth, extension-coupled operability
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:2438:19085:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s checked-out repo does not prove backend schema details; Public already runs a canonical multi-index SQLite design; basename-derived collection scoping is weaker than Public’s row-level identity model; Public’s lineage/projection tables are materially richer than Mnemosyne’s visible CRUD contract; any future binary/plugin facade needs explicit health and schema introspection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:2449:19140:- Questions addressed: schema visibility, scope keying, canonical row store vs collection store, vector and FTS persistence, lineage and deletion semantics, companion-table breadth, extension-coupled operability
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:2450:19141:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s checked-out repo does not prove backend schema details; Public already runs a canonical multi-index SQLite design; basename-derived collection scoping is weaker than Public’s row-level identity model; Public’s lineage/projection tables are materially richer than Mnemosyne’s visible CRUD contract; any future binary/plugin facade needs explicit health and schema introspection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:3667:209135:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6939:51148:- Questions addressed: agent-agnostic plugin shape, passive capture vs manual capture, multi-agent safety, project/global scoping ergonomics, AGENTS guidance, compaction survival, hybrid-search boundary, CocoIndex separation, offline/local integration assumptions.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:3668:209136:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6940:51149:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper is intentionally minimal and portable, but it delegates nearly all safe behavior to agent discipline; it has no passive capture or actor-scoped governance, its best transferable idea is the repeated memory-discipline checklist across AGENTS and compaction, and its project-first/global-lazy ergonomics are useful even though the underlying scope model is too weak for Public.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:3674:209142:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6950:51230:- Questions addressed: agent-agnostic plugin shape, passive capture vs manual capture, multi-agent safety, project/global scoping ergonomics, AGENTS guidance, compaction survival, hybrid-search boundary, CocoIndex separation, offline/local integration assumptions.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:3680:209148:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6959:51282:- Questions addressed: agent-agnostic plugin shape, passive capture vs manual capture, multi-agent safety, project/global scoping ergonomics, AGENTS guidance, compaction survival, hybrid-search boundary, CocoIndex separation, offline/local integration assumptions.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:3729:209232:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7150:55800:- Questions addressed: wrapper-visible hybrid-search contract, query handoff opacity, retrieval scope ergonomics, session-aware continuity, compaction-time retrieval guidance, package/audit boundary, offline readiness and failure handling.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:3730:209233:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7151:55801:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne is not a retrieval-engine upgrade over Public; it is a thin offline plugin façade whose strongest retrieval lessons are ergonomic and operational. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:3771:209285:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7261:58208:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:59:- Questions addressed: tool registration, CLI bridge semantics, search/store/delete verbs, project vs global scoping, compaction prompt injection, profile separation, offline-first operational surface, Public overlap boundaries
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:3772:209286:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7262:58209:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:60:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s checked-out repo proves a thin OpenCode plugin facade over a CLI binary; the real hybrid-search mechanics remain README-level; Public already exceeds it on MCP breadth, layered routing, and profile-aware recovery; the strongest reusable idea is direct memory-tool guidance during compaction, not the backend contract itself; the strict validation command was run and reported `RESULT: PASSED` despite a read-only temp-file warning from the validator shell
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:3798:209312:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7382:58559:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:5566:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper proves only a thin search bridge; its hybrid ranking claims are README-level; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval with query routing, lexical weighting, adaptive fusion, and fallback tiers; the most transferable search idea is simpler project/global memory tool ergonomics, not the backend retrieval stack; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:3800:209314:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7384:58567:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:5648:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper proves only a thin search bridge; its hybrid ranking claims are README-level; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval with query routing, lexical weighting, adaptive fusion, and fallback tiers; the most transferable search idea is simpler project/global memory tool ergonomics, not the backend retrieval stack; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:3850:209364:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7525:58803:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5781:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper exposes no dedup, no upsert, no revision lineage, no soft-delete lifecycle, and no retention/GC contract; contradiction hygiene is primarily agent guidance plus hard delete; basename-derived project scoping is materially less stable than Public’s canonical-path plus logical-key approach; the compaction reminder block is the only clear hygiene improvement worth borrowing immediately; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:3852:209366:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7530:58816:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5868:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper exposes no dedup, no upsert, no revision lineage, no soft-delete lifecycle, and no retention/GC contract; contradiction hygiene is primarily agent guidance plus hard delete; basename-derived project scoping is materially less stable than Public’s canonical-path plus logical-key approach; the compaction reminder block is the only clear hygiene improvement worth borrowing immediately; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:3857:209371:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7536:58825:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:41:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper is intentionally minimal and portable, but it delegates nearly all safe behavior to agent discipline; it has no passive capture or actor-scoped governance, its best transferable idea is the repeated memory-discipline checklist across AGENTS and compaction, and its project-first/global-lazy ergonomics are useful even though the underlying scope model is too weak for Public.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:4054:209639:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8171:79908:- Questions addressed: compaction-specific memory guidance, project-vs-global ergonomics, direct memory capture, write-time pinning, offline readiness, AGENTS guidance location, overlap with existing hybrid retrieval and community-search fallback.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:4055:209640:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8172:79909:- Questions answered: Public already owns the heavy hybrid retrieval, trigger recall, health, and governance story. The real missing surface area is ergonomic: a compaction memory action card, explicit scoped lanes, inline capture, write-time pinning, and proactive warmup. Mnemosyne’s most transferable value is its thin agent-facing contract, not its ranking backend. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:4139:209758:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8537:93503:- Questions answered: The strongest paradigm shifts are not new ranking algorithms. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:4331:210011:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9191:125437:- Questions answered: the safest near-term adoption is still the compaction reminder pattern, but only as a deduped add-on to existing compaction transport; AGENTS guidance should be rewritten for governed saves instead of copied; project/global and `core=true` need wrapper-only prototypes because Public’s scope and constitutional semantics are stricter than Mnemosyne’s; a native OpenCode facade is viable only if it delegates to MCP and never becomes a second memory authority. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:4383:210081:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9358:131110:- Questions addressed: compaction-survival leverage, governed mutation mismatch, project/global ergonomics, AGENTS/startup reuse, `core=true` semantics, OpenCode plugin layering, offline-first tradeoffs, current hybrid-retrieval overlap, CocoIndex separation.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:4384:210082:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9359:131111:- Questions answered: Phase A adopt now: add a deduped memory-tool reminder to the existing compaction transport, and rewrite startup/AGENTS wording around `session_bootstrap` plus governed save flows. Phase B prototype later: add read-only `project/global` recall aliases that compile down to current retrieval scope, with no write aliases. Phase C hold or reject: do not add a direct `core=true` shortcut, do not replace Public’s retrieval stack with Mnemosyne, and only pursue an OpenCode-native facade if it delegates to MCP instead of becoming a second authority.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:4487:210284:- Questions addressed: compaction-specific memory guidance, project-vs-global ergonomics, direct memory capture, write-time pinning, offline readiness, AGENTS guidance location, overlap with existing hybrid retrieval and community-search fallback.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:4488:210285:- Questions answered: Public already owns the heavy hybrid retrieval, trigger recall, health, and governance story. The real missing surface area is ergonomic: a compaction memory action card, explicit scoped lanes, inline capture, write-time pinning, and proactive warmup. Mnemosyne’s most transferable value is its thin agent-facing contract, not its ranking backend. The exact strict validation command passed with `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, while also emitting the read-only warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`. No `AGENTS.md` file was present under the external checkout; that guidance exists only in the README.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:6163:- Questions answered: Q1 should deliver compaction guidance plus measurement; Q2 should deliver audited inline ingest; Q2/Q3 should prototype ergonomic scope aliases; Q3 should improve operator readiness only after metrics exist.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:6164:- Novelty justification: This pass adds little new source discovery, but it converts the final recommendation set into an execution order that preserves Public’s governance model while maximizing measurable early wins.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:6173:- What I would do differently: for iteration 32, I would convert each Q1/Q2/Q3 milestone into packet-ready success criteria and explicit verification gates so the roadmap can be executed without re-arguing sequencing.

Phase B prototype later: add read-only `project/global` recall aliases that compile down to current retrieval scope, with no write aliases. Phase C hold or reject: do not add a direct `core=true` shortcut, do not replace Public’s retrieval stack with Mnemosyne, and only pursue an OpenCode-native facade if it delegates to MCP instead of becoming a second authority.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:1924:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5586:- Questions answered: Phase A adopt now: add a deduped memory-tool reminder to the existing compaction transport, and rewrite startup/AGENTS wording around `session_bootstrap` plus governed save flows. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:3992:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is operationally richer, but it lacks a proactive “make memory ready now” surface. A `memory_warmup` or `memory_doctor` feature that preloads providers, validates embedding profile, primes indices, and returns deterministic ready/not-ready output would remove a lot of first-query uncertainty.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:4010:## Assessment
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:4011:- New information ratio: 0.79
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:4012:- Questions addressed: compaction-specific memory guidance, project-vs-global ergonomics, direct memory capture, write-time pinning, offline readiness, AGENTS guidance location, overlap with existing hybrid retrieval and community-search fallback.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:4013:- Questions answered: Public already owns the heavy hybrid retrieval, trigger recall, health, and governance story. The real missing surface area is ergonomic: a compaction memory action card, explicit scoped lanes, inline capture, write-time pinning, and proactive warmup. Mnemosyne’s most transferable value is its thin agent-facing contract, not its ranking backend. The exact strict validation command passed with `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, while also emitting the read-only warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`. No `AGENTS.md` file was present under the external checkout; that guidance exists only in the README.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:4015:## Reflection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:4017:- What did not work: The sandbox is read-only, so I could not update `research/research.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, or save memory for the phase. The external repo also does not expose Mnemosyne’s Go internals, so anything below the wrapper remains README-level contract rather than source-proven implementation.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:4019:## Recommended Next Focus
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:4062:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is operationally richer, but it lacks a proactive “make memory ready now” surface. A `memory_warmup` or `memory_doctor` feature that preloads providers, validates embedding profile, primes indices, and returns deterministic ready/not-ready output would remove a lot of first-query uncertainty.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:4156:## Assessment
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:4158:- New information ratio: 0.46
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:4159:- Questions addressed: hybrid contract vs wrapper proof, compaction resilience, AGENTS/startup reuse, project/global ergonomics, scope identity stability, inline save ergonomics, constitutional pinning semantics, mutation/audit model, OpenCode facade design, federated retrieval separation, offline readiness tradeoffs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:4160:- Questions answered: Public should borrow Mnemosyne’s behavioral ergonomics, not its authority model. The safe path is `session_bootstrap`-aligned guidance plus a transport-owned compaction memory card, with any future aliases or facades compiled into existing MCP/governance surfaces.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:4163:## Reflection

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:4167:## Recommended Next Focus

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:49:- Questions answered: the wrapper is thin; hybrid ranking is README-level not wrapper-owned; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval; project/global split is useful but collision-prone; compaction tool reminders are the best adopt-now idea; CocoIndex should stay separate from memory retrieval

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:12885:- New information ratio: 0.86
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:12886:- Questions addressed: schema visibility, scope keying, canonical row store vs collection store, vector and FTS persistence, lineage and deletion semantics, companion-table breadth, extension-coupled operability
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:12887:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s checked-out repo does not prove backend schema details; Public already runs a canonical multi-index SQLite design; basename-derived collection scoping is weaker than Public’s row-level identity model; Public’s lineage/projection tables are materially richer than Mnemosyne’s visible CRUD contract; any future binary/plugin facade needs explicit health and schema introspection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:12889:## Reflection

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:12893:## Recommended Next Focus

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:68:- Questions addressed: retrieval-plane boundary, project vs global scoping ergonomics, runtime routing enforcement, AGENTS guidance reuse, contract drift between handler and schema
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:69:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne confirms the value of a dedicated memory plane, but Public’s stronger design is federation by task intent. Public already exceeds Mnemosyne inside the memory plane through `retrievalLevel` and community fallback. The most actionable gap is not backend ranking work; it is surfacing that memory-plane contract consistently in the shared schema and OpenCode-facing UX. The exact validation command passed with `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, while still emitting `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:71:## Reflection

The phase prompt’s nested `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main` path also does not match the actual checkout, which is rooted directly at `external/`.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11619:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s failure model is thin and backend-dependent. The wrapper mitigates one narrow edge case well, query quoting, but otherwise lacks readiness, migration, corruption, and repair contracts. Public is materially stronger here because it exposes degraded states, recovery tooling, bounded repair, and startup integrity checks. The exact validation command used was `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh "/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main" --strict`, which returned `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED` while also emitting `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:6781:- Questions answered: the riskiest migrations are contract collisions, not algorithm swaps; every adopt-now item needs an explicit kill switch and a no-data-loss rollback.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:57:- New information ratio: 0.81
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:58:- Questions addressed: missing-binary handling, first-model-download readiness, corruption and repair visibility, rename and scope migration risk, delete hygiene, compaction behavior under failure, wrapper-level scaling limits
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:59:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s failure model is thin and backend-dependent. The wrapper mitigates one narrow edge case well, query quoting, but otherwise lacks readiness, migration, corruption, and repair contracts. Public is materially stronger here because it exposes degraded states, recovery tooling, bounded repair, and startup integrity checks. The exact validation command used was `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh "/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main" --strict`, which returned `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED` while also emitting `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:5930:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:1901:- Questions addressed: hybrid contract vs wrapper proof, compaction resilience, AGENTS/startup reuse, project/global ergonomics, scope identity stability, inline save ergonomics, constitutional pinning semantics, mutation/audit model, OpenCode facade design, federated retrieval separation, offline readiness tradeoffs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:5944:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:2011:- Questions addressed: hybrid contract vs wrapper proof, compaction resilience, AGENTS/startup reuse, project/global ergonomics, scope identity stability, inline save ergonomics, constitutional pinning semantics, mutation/audit model, OpenCode facade design, federated retrieval separation, offline readiness tradeoffs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:6566:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:46:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s lifecycle is storage-first and instruction-driven; Public’s lifecycle is session-first and contract-driven. The strongest reusable ideas are lightweight project/global ergonomics and optional compaction reminders, but Mnemosyne’s stateless tool boundary, basename-derived identity, and string-only session save model should not replace `memory_context`, `generate-context.js`, or `session_bootstrap`. The exact strict validation command passed with `RESULT: PASSED` and `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0`, alongside the expected read-only warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:6929:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6208:- Questions addressed: hygiene enforcement versus guidance, trigger-based recall, mutation safety, contradiction handling, operational health/repair, packaging boundary and external repo thinness, AGENTS guidance location, offline-first readiness cost.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:6935:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6288:- Questions addressed: hygiene enforcement versus guidance, trigger-based recall, mutation safety, contradiction handling, operational health/repair, packaging boundary and external repo thinness, AGENTS guidance location, offline-first readiness cost.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:7183:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6939:51148:- Questions addressed: agent-agnostic plugin shape, passive capture vs manual capture, multi-agent safety, project/global scoping ergonomics, AGENTS guidance, compaction survival, hybrid-search boundary, CocoIndex separation, offline/local integration assumptions.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:7184:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6940:51149:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper is intentionally minimal and portable, but it delegates nearly all safe behavior to agent discipline; it has no passive capture or actor-scoped governance, its best transferable idea is the repeated memory-discipline checklist across AGENTS and compaction, and its project-first/global-lazy ergonomics are useful even though the underlying scope model is too weak for Public.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:7190:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6950:51230:- Questions addressed: agent-agnostic plugin shape, passive capture vs manual capture, multi-agent safety, project/global scoping ergonomics, AGENTS guidance, compaction survival, hybrid-search boundary, CocoIndex separation, offline/local integration assumptions.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:7334:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7262:58209:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:60:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s checked-out repo proves a thin OpenCode plugin facade over a CLI binary; the real hybrid-search mechanics remain README-level; Public already exceeds it on MCP breadth, layered routing, and profile-aware recovery; the strongest reusable idea is direct memory-tool guidance during compaction, not the backend contract itself; the strict validation command was run and reported `RESULT: PASSED` despite a read-only temp-file warning from the validator shell
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:7360:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7382:58559:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:5566:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper proves only a thin search bridge; its hybrid ranking claims are README-level; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval with query routing, lexical weighting, adaptive fusion, and fallback tiers; the most transferable search idea is simpler project/global memory tool ergonomics, not the backend retrieval stack; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:7362:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7384:58567:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:5648:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper proves only a thin search bridge; its hybrid ranking claims are README-level; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval with query routing, lexical weighting, adaptive fusion, and fallback tiers; the most transferable search idea is simpler project/global memory tool ergonomics, not the backend retrieval stack; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:7389:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7470:58707:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5993:- Questions answered: the wrapper is thin; hybrid ranking is README-level not wrapper-owned; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval; project/global split is useful but collision-prone; compaction tool reminders are the best adopt-now idea; CocoIndex should stay separate from memory retrieval

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:7412:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7525:58803:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5781:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper exposes no dedup, no upsert, no revision lineage, no soft-delete lifecycle, and no retention/GC contract; contradiction hygiene is primarily agent guidance plus hard delete; basename-derived project scoping is materially less stable than Public’s canonical-path plus logical-key approach; the compaction reminder block is the only clear hygiene improvement worth borrowing immediately; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:7481:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7725:59275:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:49:- Questions answered: the wrapper is thin; hybrid ranking is README-level not wrapper-owned; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval; project/global split is useful but collision-prone; compaction tool reminders are the best adopt-now idea; CocoIndex should stay separate from memory retrieval
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:7509:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7806:59562:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6982:- Questions addressed: tool registration, CLI bridge semantics, search/store/delete verbs, project vs global scoping, compaction prompt injection, profile separation, offline-first operational surface, Public overlap boundaries

The exact strict validation command passed with `RESULT: PASSED` and `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0`, alongside the expected read-only warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:8129:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9358:131110:- Questions addressed: compaction-survival leverage, governed mutation mismatch, project/global ergonomics, AGENTS/startup reuse, `core=true` semantics, OpenCode plugin layering, offline-first tradeoffs, current hybrid-retrieval overlap, CocoIndex separation.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:8130:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9359:131111:- Questions answered: Phase A adopt now: add a deduped memory-tool reminder to the existing compaction transport, and rewrite startup/AGENTS wording around `session_bootstrap` plus governed save flows. Phase B prototype later: add read-only `project/global` recall aliases that compile down to current retrieval scope, with no write aliases. Phase C hold or reject: do not add a direct `core=true` shortcut, do not replace Public’s retrieval stack with Mnemosyne, and only pursue an OpenCode-native facade if it delegates to MCP instead of becoming a second authority.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:8267:135615:- Questions addressed: hybrid contract vs wrapper proof, compaction resilience, AGENTS/startup reuse, project/global ergonomics, scope identity stability, inline save ergonomics, constitutional pinning semantics, mutation/audit model, OpenCode facade design, federated retrieval separation, offline readiness tradeoffs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:8268:135616:- Questions answered: Public should borrow Mnemosyne’s behavioral ergonomics, not its authority model. The safe path is `session_bootstrap`-aligned guidance plus a transport-owned compaction memory card, with any future aliases or facades compiled into existing MCP/governance surfaces.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:8278:135725:- Questions addressed: hybrid contract vs wrapper proof, compaction resilience, AGENTS/startup reuse, project/global ergonomics, scope identity stability, inline save ergonomics, constitutional pinning semantics, mutation/audit model, OpenCode facade design, federated retrieval separation, offline readiness tradeoffs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:8279:135726:- Questions answered: Public should borrow Mnemosyne’s behavioral ergonomics, not its authority model. The safe path is `session_bootstrap`-aligned guidance plus a transport-owned compaction memory card, with any future aliases or facades compiled into existing MCP/governance surfaces.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:8312:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is operationally richer, but it lacks a proactive “make memory ready now” surface. A `memory_warmup` or `memory_doctor` feature that preloads providers, validates embedding profile, primes indices, and returns deterministic ready/not-ready output would remove a lot of first-query uncertainty.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:8330:## Assessment
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:8331:- New information ratio: 0.79
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:8332:- Questions addressed: compaction-specific memory guidance, project-vs-global ergonomics, direct memory capture, write-time pinning, offline readiness, AGENTS guidance location, overlap with existing hybrid retrieval and community-search fallback.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:8333:- Questions answered: Public already owns the heavy hybrid retrieval, trigger recall, health, and governance story. The real missing surface area is ergonomic: a compaction memory action card, explicit scoped lanes, inline capture, write-time pinning, and proactive warmup. Mnemosyne’s most transferable value is its thin agent-facing contract, not its ranking backend. The exact strict validation command passed with `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, while also emitting the read-only warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`. No `AGENTS.md` file was present under the external checkout; that guidance exists only in the README.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:8335:## Reflection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:8337:- What did not work: The sandbox is read-only, so I could not update `research/research.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, or save memory for the phase. Call at the start of implementation work to record knowledge, uncertainty, and context scores for learning measurement. Token Budget: 1200.',
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:8563:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:473:  inputSchema: { type: 'object', additionalProperties: false, properties: { specFolder: { type: 'string', minLength: 1, description: 'Path to spec folder (e.g., "specs/003-memory/077-upgrade")' }, taskId: { type: 'string', minLength: 1, description: 'Task identifier (e.g., "T1", "T2", "implementation")' }, knowledgeScore: { type: 'number', minimum: 0, maximum: 100, description: 'Current knowledge level (0-100): How well do you understand the task requirements and codebase context?' }, uncertaintyScore: { type: 'number', minimum: 0, maximum: 100, description: 'Current uncertainty level (0-100): How uncertain are you about the approach or implementation?' }, contextScore: { type: 'number', minimum: 0, maximum: 100, description: 'Current context completeness (0-100): How complete is your understanding of relevant context?' }, knowledgeGaps: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'List of identified knowledge gaps (optional)' }, sessionId: { type: 'string', description: 'Optional session identifier' } }, required: ['specFolder', 'taskId', 'knowledgeScore', 'uncertaintyScore', 'contextScore'] },
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:8564:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:479:  inputSchema: { type: 'object', additionalProperties: false, properties: { specFolder: { type: 'string', minLength: 1, description: 'Path to spec folder (must match preflight)' }, taskId: { type: 'string', minLength: 1, description: 'Task identifier (must match preflight)' }, knowledgeScore: { type: 'number', minimum: 0, maximum: 100, description: 'Post-task knowledge level (0-100)' }, uncertaintyScore: { type: 'number', minimum: 0, maximum: 100, description: 'Post-task uncertainty level (0-100)' }, contextScore: { type: 'number', minimum: 0, maximum: 100, description: 'Post-task context completeness (0-100)' }, gapsClosed: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'List of knowledge gaps closed during task (optional)' }, newGapsDiscovered: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'List of new gaps discovered during task (optional)' }, sessionId: { type: 'string', description: 'Optional session identifier. Required when multiple sessions share the same taskId and you need to target a specific learning cycle.' } }, required: ['specFolder', 'taskId', 'knowledgeScore', 'uncertaintyScore', 'contextScore'] },

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:48:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne is not a retrieval-engine upgrade over Public; it is a thin offline plugin façade whose strongest retrieval lessons are ergonomic and operational. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:50:## Reflection
