.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:2310:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:60:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s checked-out repo proves a thin OpenCode plugin facade over a CLI binary; the real hybrid-search mechanics remain README-level; Public already exceeds it on MCP breadth, layered routing, and profile-aware recovery; the strongest reusable idea is direct memory-tool guidance during compaction, not the backend contract itself; the strict validation command was run and reported `RESULT: PASSED` despite a read-only temp-file warning from the validator shell
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:2573:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8231:- Questions addressed: implicit versus explicit session creation, session maintenance state, stop-time summarization, compaction survival, trusted session identity, stale-session cleanup, Public hybrid retrieval overlap

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:2903:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5780:- Questions addressed: dedup/upsert behavior, revision tracking, delete semantics, garbage collection, soft deletes, topic key stability, compaction hygiene guidance, auditability of mutation flows
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:2904:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5781:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper exposes no dedup, no upsert, no revision lineage, no soft-delete lifecycle, and no retention/GC contract; contradiction hygiene is primarily agent guidance plus hard delete; basename-derived project scoping is materially less stable than Public’s canonical-path plus logical-key approach; the compaction reminder block is the only clear hygiene improvement worth borrowing immediately; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:2917:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5868:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper exposes no dedup, no upsert, no revision lineage, no soft-delete lifecycle, and no retention/GC contract; contradiction hygiene is primarily agent guidance plus hard delete; basename-derived project scoping is materially less stable than Public’s canonical-path plus logical-key approach; the compaction reminder block is the only clear hygiene improvement worth borrowing immediately; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:2926:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:41:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper is intentionally minimal and portable, but it delegates nearly all safe behavior to agent discipline; it has no passive capture or actor-scoped governance, its best transferable idea is the repeated memory-discipline checklist across AGENTS and compaction, and its project-first/global-lazy ergonomics are useful even though the underlying scope model is too weak for Public.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:4644:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s lifecycle is storage-first and instruction-driven; Public’s lifecycle is session-first and contract-driven. The strongest reusable ideas are lightweight project/global ergonomics and optional compaction reminders, but Mnemosyne’s stateless tool boundary, basename-derived identity, and string-only session save model should not replace `memory_context`, `generate-context.js`, or `session_bootstrap`. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:40:- Questions addressed: agent-agnostic plugin shape, passive capture vs manual capture, multi-agent safety, project/global scoping ergonomics, AGENTS guidance, compaction survival, hybrid-search boundary, CocoIndex separation, offline/local integration assumptions.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:41:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper is intentionally minimal and portable, but it delegates nearly all safe behavior to agent discipline; it has no passive capture or actor-scoped governance, its best transferable idea is the repeated memory-discipline checklist across AGENTS and compaction, and its project-first/global-lazy ergonomics are useful even though the underlying scope model is too weak for Public.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:47:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper exposes no dedup, no upsert, no revision lineage, no soft-delete lifecycle, and no retention/GC contract; contradiction hygiene is primarily agent guidance plus hard delete; basename-derived project scoping is materially less stable than Public’s canonical-path plus logical-key approach; the compaction reminder block is the only clear hygiene improvement worth borrowing immediately; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:49:## Reflection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:51:- What did not work: the external repo is intentionally minimal, so many lifecycle questions stop at wrapper absence rather than deeper source proof; this sandbox is read-only, so I could not update `research/research.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js` to save phase memory
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:53:## Recommended Next Focus

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:361:    13	4. **HALT:** Stop immediately if uncertain, if line numbers don't match, or if tests fail. (See "Halt Conditions" below).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:365:    17	- **Never lie or fabricate** - use "UNKNOWN" when uncertain.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:367:    19	- **Use explicit uncertainty:** Prefix claims with "I'M UNCERTAIN ABOUT THIS:".
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:465:   117	3. Parse request → Check confidence AND uncertainty (see §4)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:466:   118	4. **Dual-threshold:** confidence ≥ 0.70 AND uncertainty ≤ 0.35 → PROCEED. Either fails → INVESTIGATE (max 3 iterations) → ESCALATE. Simple: <40% ASK | 40-69% CAUTION | ≥70% PASS
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5109:## Assessment
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5110:- New information ratio: 0.84
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5111:- Questions addressed: agent-agnostic plugin shape, passive capture vs manual capture, multi-agent safety, project/global scoping ergonomics, AGENTS guidance, compaction survival, hybrid-search boundary, CocoIndex separation, offline/local integration assumptions.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5112:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper is intentionally minimal and portable, but it delegates nearly all safe behavior to agent discipline; it has no passive capture or actor-scoped governance, its best transferable idea is the repeated memory-discipline checklist across AGENTS and compaction, and its project-first/global-lazy ergonomics are useful even though the underlying scope model is too weak for Public.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5115:## Reflection

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5119:## Recommended Next Focus

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-028.md:15652:## Assessment
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-028.md:15653:- New information ratio: 0.68
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-028.md:15661:## Recommended Next Focus
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:18:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is operationally richer, but it lacks a proactive “make memory ready now” surface. A `memory_warmup` or `memory_doctor` feature that preloads providers, validates embedding profile, primes indices, and returns deterministic ready/not-ready output would remove a lot of first-query uncertainty.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:36:## Assessment
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:37:- New information ratio: 0.79
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:38:- Questions addressed: compaction-specific memory guidance, project-vs-global ergonomics, direct memory capture, write-time pinning, offline readiness, AGENTS guidance location, overlap with existing hybrid retrieval and community-search fallback.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:39:- Questions answered: Public already owns the heavy hybrid retrieval, trigger recall, health, and governance story. The real missing surface area is ergonomic: a compaction memory action card, explicit scoped lanes, inline capture, write-time pinning, and proactive warmup. Mnemosyne’s most transferable value is its thin agent-facing contract, not its ranking backend. The exact strict validation command passed with `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, while also emitting the read-only warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`. No `AGENTS.md` file was present under the external checkout; that guidance exists only in the README.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6648:- New information ratio: 0.81

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6650:- Questions answered: The strongest paradigm shifts are not new ranking algorithms. They are API and runtime shifts: compaction should preserve memory behavior, retrieval posture must be explicit in the tool contract, pinning should be separated from governance tiers, OpenCode should get a curated facade over the governed MCP core, and memory retrieval should remain one plane in a federated routing stack. The exact validation command passed with `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, while still emitting `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:46:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s checked-out repo does not prove backend schema details; Public already runs a canonical multi-index SQLite design; basename-derived collection scoping is weaker than Public’s row-level identity model; Public’s lineage/projection tables are materially richer than Mnemosyne’s visible CRUD contract; any future binary/plugin facade needs explicit health and schema introspection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:48:## Reflection

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:3429:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/iteration-1.md:35:- Phase 005 open questions note that `external/plugins/claude-memory/CLAUDE.md` does not exist in this checkout.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:3501:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:65:- Component A vs Component B: honest reporting wants uncertainty exposed; pricing normalization currently uses fallback defaults that can hide uncertainty unless the dashboard surfaces that status explicitly. [SOURCE: 003-contextador/external/src/lib/core/stats.ts:98-101] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/skills/get-token-insights/scripts/ingest_token_data.py:72-96]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6980:## Assessment
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6981:- New information ratio: 0.84
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6982:- Questions addressed: tool registration, CLI bridge semantics, search/store/delete verbs, project vs global scoping, compaction prompt injection, profile separation, offline-first operational surface, Public overlap boundaries
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6983:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s checked-out repo proves a thin OpenCode plugin facade over a CLI binary; the real hybrid-search mechanics remain README-level; Public already exceeds it on MCP breadth, layered routing, and profile-aware recovery; the strongest reusable idea is direct memory-tool guidance during compaction, not the backend contract itself; the strict validation command was run and reported `RESULT: PASSED` despite a read-only temp-file warning from the validator shell
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6985:## Reflection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6987:- What did not work: the external repo does not include Mnemosyne backend internals, so BM25/vector/RRF remain documented claims only; this sandbox is read-only, so I could not update the phase artifacts on disk during this iteration
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6989:## Recommended Next Focus

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:7067:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s checked-out repo proves a thin OpenCode plugin facade over a CLI binary; the real hybrid-search mechanics remain README-level; Public already exceeds it on MCP breadth, layered routing, and profile-aware recovery; the strongest reusable idea is direct memory-tool guidance during compaction, not the backend contract itself; the strict validation command was run and reported `RESULT: PASSED` despite a read-only temp-file warning from the validator shell
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:7069:## Reflection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:7071:- What did not work: the external repo does not include Mnemosyne backend internals, so BM25/vector/RRF remain documented claims only; this sandbox is read-only, so I could not update the phase artifacts on disk during this iteration
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:7073:## Recommended Next Focus
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:37:## Assessment
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:38:- New information ratio: 0.81
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:39:- Questions addressed: hygiene enforcement versus guidance, trigger-based recall, mutation safety, contradiction handling, operational health/repair, packaging boundary and external repo thinness, AGENTS guidance location, offline-first readiness cost.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:40:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s memory hygiene is wrapper-thin and agent-disciplined; Public’s hygiene is system-disciplined through save quality gates, trigger recall, causal lineage, mutation ledgers, and repair tooling. The best transferable idea is a short Mnemosyne-style hygiene reminder layered onto our existing compaction/startup surfaces, not any simplification of our save/delete/health/trigger contracts. The exact strict validation command passed with `RESULT: PASSED` and `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0`, while also emitting the expected read-only warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:42:## Reflection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:44:- What did not work: the MCP memory/CocoIndex helper calls were not usable in this pass, so I fell back to direct file tracing with `rg` and `sed`. The sandbox is read-only, so I could not update `research/research.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js` to save phase memory.

Required when multiple sessions share the same taskId and you need to target a specific learning cycle.' } }, required: ['specFolder', 'taskId', 'knowledgeScore', 'uncertaintyScore', 'contextScore'] },

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:12959:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is operationally richer, but it lacks a proactive “make memory ready now” surface. A `memory_warmup` or `memory_doctor` feature that preloads providers, validates embedding profile, primes indices, and returns deterministic ready/not-ready output would remove a lot of first-query uncertainty.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:12977:## Assessment
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:12978:- New information ratio: 0.79
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:12979:- Questions addressed: compaction-specific memory guidance, project-vs-global ergonomics, direct memory capture, write-time pinning, offline readiness, AGENTS guidance location, overlap with existing hybrid retrieval and community-search fallback.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:12980:- Questions answered: Public already owns the heavy hybrid retrieval, trigger recall, health, and governance story. The real missing surface area is ergonomic: a compaction memory action card, explicit scoped lanes, inline capture, write-time pinning, and proactive warmup. Mnemosyne’s most transferable value is its thin agent-facing contract, not its ranking backend. The exact strict validation command passed with `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, while also emitting the read-only warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`. No `AGENTS.md` file was present under the external checkout; that guidance exists only in the README.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:12982:## Reflection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:12984:- What did not work: The sandbox is read-only, so I could not update `research/research.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, or save memory for the phase. The external repo also does not expose Mnemosyne’s Go internals, so anything below the wrapper remains README-level contract rather than source-proven implementation.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:12986:## Recommended Next Focus
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:13029:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is operationally richer, but it lacks a proactive “make memory ready now” surface. A `memory_warmup` or `memory_doctor` feature that preloads providers, validates embedding profile, primes indices, and returns deterministic ready/not-ready output would remove a lot of first-query uncertainty.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:13047:## Assessment

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:399:## Recommended Next Focus
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1131:4. **HALT:** Stop immediately if uncertain, if line numbers don't match, or if tests fail. (See "Halt Conditions" below).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1135:- **Never lie or fabricate** - use "UNKNOWN" when uncertain.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1137:- **Use explicit uncertainty:** Prefix claims with "I'M UNCERTAIN ABOUT THIS:".
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1235:3. Parse request → Check confidence AND uncertainty (see §4)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1236:4. **Dual-threshold:** confidence ≥ 0.70 AND uncertainty ≤ 0.35 → PROCEED. Either fails → INVESTIGATE (max 3 iterations) → ESCALATE. Simple: <40% ASK | 40-69% CAUTION | ≥70% PASS
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3151:## Assessment
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3152:- New information ratio: 0.74
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3153:- Questions addressed: strongest end-to-end pattern; how Mex turns markdown into auditable claims; whether targeted sync is meaningfully different from our current health/save surfaces; whether repo-activity freshness is a distinct signal; whether omission detection adds value beyond structural validation.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3154:- Questions answered: the strongest pattern is a separate doc-truth control plane; targeted sync is the highest-value missing capability for us; claim extraction is the technical hinge that makes the plane possible; dual-signal freshness is worth adding as a new axis; omission checks are useful, but Mex’s exact heuristics should be treated as prototypes, not copied wholesale.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3156:## Reflection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3158:- What did not work: the prompt’s `external/mex-main` path is stale in this checkout, the real snapshot is `external/`; the strict validator passed, but it still emitted `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`, so the pass signal was clean but noisy.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3160:## Recommended Next Focus
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3257:## Assessment
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3258:- New information ratio: 0.76
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3259:- Questions addressed: status/bootstrap behavior, wake-up layering, forced-save timing, crash/session state, save-path authority
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3260:- Questions answered: MemPalace’s best lifecycle idea is enforced pre-loss saving; Public already exceeds it on recovery depth, state trust, and structured save semantics; the main reusable piece is concise bootstrap guidance, not a retrieval/state reset
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3262:## Reflection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3264:- What did not work: the requested phase prerequisites are not present because `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `checklist.md`, and `decision-record.md` are all missing in `005-mempalace`; the strict validator still returned `RESULT: PASSED` while also emitting `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`; and I could not run the exact write-based deep-research/save workflow in this read-only sandbox
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3266:## Recommended Next Focus
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3380:## Assessment
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3381:- New information ratio: 0.74
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3382:- Questions addressed: hook timing feasibility, protocol/bootstrap portability, wake-up layering fit, taxonomy integration cost, temporal KG separation, benchmark truthfulness boundaries, Public overlap analysis
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3383:- Questions answered: the most feasible MemPalace transfers are compaction-time preservation and status-level behavior guidance; the least attractive is full palace taxonomy; the highest-upside but hardest item is a separate governed temporal fact plane
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3385:## Reflection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3387:- What did not work: this environment is read-only, the phase folder is still missing the expected Level 3 docs, and the validator reported `PASSED` while also emitting `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`, so I could not produce compliant packet artifacts or trust the validation signal as a full closeout gate
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3389:## Recommended Next Focus

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:5732:## Assessment
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:5733:- New information ratio: **0.76**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:5734:- Questions addressed: whether Modus’s non-search modules imply a different memory architecture; whether Public’s largest remaining gaps are workflow/governance rather than retrieval; whether trust stages, PRs, and missions form a true control plane; whether decay should become an operator-visible state machine.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:5735:- Questions answered: **yes** — the strongest paradigm shifts are **multi-plane memory architecture, autonomy-stage governance, proposal-first mutation, decay-as-workflow, and readiness-aware mission state**. The delta is now more about **memory operations architecture** than about BM25 or FSRS formulas.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:5737:## Reflection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:5739:- What did not work: Looking at beliefs/entities in isolation was too low-level; the paradigm shift only became visible once the operator-facing surfaces were included. Search-only comparisons would have understated the real gap.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:5741:## Recommended Next Focus
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:5983:6005:- Questions addressed: hybrid retrieval boundary, model/runtime implications, project-vs-global scoping, compaction survival, AGENTS guidance, CocoIndex separation, plugin-vs-MCP adoption shape, offline-first tradeoffs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:5984:6006:- Questions answered: the wrapper is thin; hybrid ranking is README-level not wrapper-owned; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval; project/global split is useful but collision-prone; compaction tool reminders are the best adopt-now idea; CocoIndex should stay separate from memory retrieval
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:5996:6092:- Questions addressed: hybrid retrieval boundary, model/runtime implications, project-vs-global scoping, compaction survival, AGENTS guidance, CocoIndex separation, plugin-vs-MCP adoption shape, offline-first tradeoffs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:5997:6093:- Questions answered: the wrapper is thin; hybrid ranking is README-level not wrapper-owned; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval; project/global split is useful but collision-prone; compaction tool reminders are the best adopt-now idea; CocoIndex should stay separate from memory retrieval
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6007:6153:- Questions addressed: hybrid retrieval boundary, model/runtime implications, project-vs-global scoping, compaction survival, AGENTS guidance, CocoIndex separation, plugin-vs-MCP adoption shape, offline-first tradeoffs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6008:6154:- Questions answered: the wrapper is thin; hybrid ranking is README-level not wrapper-owned; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval; project/global split is useful but collision-prone; compaction tool reminders are the best adopt-now idea; CocoIndex should stay separate from memory retrieval
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6412:26077:- Questions addressed: tool registration, CLI bridge semantics, search/store/delete verbs, project vs global scoping, compaction prompt injection, profile separation, offline-first operational surface, Public overlap boundaries
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6413:26078:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s checked-out repo proves a thin OpenCode plugin facade over a CLI binary; the real hybrid-search mechanics remain README-level; Public already exceeds it on MCP breadth, layered routing, and profile-aware recovery; the strongest reusable idea is direct memory-tool guidance during compaction, not the backend contract itself; the strict validation command was run and reported `RESULT: PASSED` despite a read-only temp-file warning from the validator shell
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6415:26082:- What did not work: the external repo does not include Mnemosyne backend internals, so BM25/vector/RRF remain documented claims only; this sandbox is read-only, so I could not update the phase artifacts on disk during this iteration
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6425:26161:- Questions addressed: tool registration, CLI bridge semantics, search/store/delete verbs, project vs global scoping, compaction prompt injection, profile separation, offline-first operational surface, Public overlap boundaries
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6426:26162:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s checked-out repo proves a thin OpenCode plugin facade over a CLI binary; the real hybrid-search mechanics remain README-level; Public already exceeds it on MCP breadth, layered routing, and profile-aware recovery; the strongest reusable idea is direct memory-tool guidance during compaction, not the backend contract itself; the strict validation command was run and reported `RESULT: PASSED` despite a read-only temp-file warning from the validator shell
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6428:26166:- What did not work: the external repo does not include Mnemosyne backend internals, so BM25/vector/RRF remain documented claims only; this sandbox is read-only, so I could not update the phase artifacts on disk during this iteration
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6437:26231:- Questions addressed: tool registration, CLI bridge semantics, search/store/delete verbs, project vs global scoping, compaction prompt injection, profile separation, offline-first operational surface, Public overlap boundaries
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6438:26232:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s checked-out repo proves a thin OpenCode plugin facade over a CLI binary; the real hybrid-search mechanics remain README-level; Public already exceeds it on MCP breadth, layered routing, and profile-aware recovery; the strongest reusable idea is direct memory-tool guidance during compaction, not the backend contract itself; the strict validation command was run and reported `RESULT: PASSED` despite a read-only temp-file warning from the validator shell
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6440:26236:- What did not work: the external repo does not include Mnemosyne backend internals, so BM25/vector/RRF remain documented claims only; this sandbox is read-only, so I could not update the phase artifacts on disk during this iteration
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6566:34403:- Questions addressed: implicit versus explicit session creation, session maintenance state, stop-time summarization, compaction survival, trusted session identity, stale-session cleanup, Public hybrid retrieval overlap
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6567:34404:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne has no first-class session object; continuity is mostly prompt discipline plus collection scoping; compaction preserves tool awareness but not live continuity state; Public already has server-minted session IDs, working-memory continuity, cached summary validation, session-stop autosave, and stale cleanup; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` despite the shell warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6571:34490:- Questions addressed: implicit versus explicit session creation, session maintenance state, stop-time summarization, compaction survival, trusted session identity, stale-session cleanup, Public hybrid retrieval overlap
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6572:34491:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne has no first-class session object; continuity is mostly prompt discipline plus collection scoping; compaction preserves tool awareness but not live continuity state; Public already has server-minted session IDs, working-memory continuity, cached summary validation, session-stop autosave, and stale cleanup; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` despite the shell warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6576:34546:- Questions addressed: implicit versus explicit session creation, session maintenance state, stop-time summarization, compaction survival, trusted session identity, stale-session cleanup, Public hybrid retrieval overlap
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6577:34547:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne has no first-class session object; continuity is mostly prompt discipline plus collection scoping; compaction preserves tool awareness but not live continuity state; Public already has server-minted session IDs, working-memory continuity, cached summary validation, session-stop autosave, and stale cleanup; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` despite the shell warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6726:40066:- Questions addressed: wrapper-visible ranking behavior, query preprocessing, FTS/BM25/vector contract, Public overlap, adaptive fusion depth, scope ergonomics, offline-first cost
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6727:40067:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper proves only a thin search bridge; its hybrid ranking claims are README-level; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval with query routing, lexical weighting, adaptive fusion, and fallback tiers; the most transferable search idea is simpler project/global memory tool ergonomics, not the backend retrieval stack; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6735:40216:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper proves only a thin search bridge; its hybrid ranking claims are README-level; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval with query routing, lexical weighting, adaptive fusion, and fallback tiers; the most transferable search idea is simpler project/global memory tool ergonomics, not the backend retrieval stack; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6797:45939:- Questions addressed: dedup/upsert behavior, revision tracking, delete semantics, garbage collection, soft deletes, topic key stability, compaction hygiene guidance, auditability of mutation flows
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6798:45940:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper exposes no dedup, no upsert, no revision lineage, no soft-delete lifecycle, and no retention/GC contract; contradiction hygiene is primarily agent guidance plus hard delete; basename-derived project scoping is materially less stable than Public’s canonical-path plus logical-key approach; the compaction reminder block is the only clear hygiene improvement worth borrowing immediately; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6802:46026:- Questions addressed: dedup/upsert behavior, revision tracking, delete semantics, garbage collection, soft deletes, topic key stability, compaction hygiene guidance, auditability of mutation flows
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6803:46027:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper exposes no dedup, no upsert, no revision lineage, no soft-delete lifecycle, and no retention/GC contract; contradiction hygiene is primarily agent guidance plus hard delete; basename-derived project scoping is materially less stable than Public’s canonical-path plus logical-key approach; the compaction reminder block is the only clear hygiene improvement worth borrowing immediately; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6807:46084:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper exposes no dedup, no upsert, no revision lineage, no soft-delete lifecycle, and no retention/GC contract; contradiction hygiene is primarily agent guidance plus hard delete; basename-derived project scoping is materially less stable than Public’s canonical-path plus logical-key approach; the compaction reminder block is the only clear hygiene improvement worth borrowing immediately; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6939:51148:- Questions addressed: agent-agnostic plugin shape, passive capture vs manual capture, multi-agent safety, project/global scoping ergonomics, AGENTS guidance, compaction survival, hybrid-search boundary, CocoIndex separation, offline/local integration assumptions.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6940:51149:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper is intentionally minimal and portable, but it delegates nearly all safe behavior to agent discipline; it has no passive capture or actor-scoped governance, its best transferable idea is the repeated memory-discipline checklist across AGENTS and compaction, and its project-first/global-lazy ergonomics are useful even though the underlying scope model is too weak for Public.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:6950:51230:- Questions addressed: agent-agnostic plugin shape, passive capture vs manual capture, multi-agent safety, project/global scoping ergonomics, AGENTS guidance, compaction survival, hybrid-search boundary, CocoIndex separation, offline/local integration assumptions.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7261:58208:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:59:- Questions addressed: tool registration, CLI bridge semantics, search/store/delete verbs, project vs global scoping, compaction prompt injection, profile separation, offline-first operational surface, Public overlap boundaries
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7262:58209:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:60:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s checked-out repo proves a thin OpenCode plugin facade over a CLI binary; the real hybrid-search mechanics remain README-level; Public already exceeds it on MCP breadth, layered routing, and profile-aware recovery; the strongest reusable idea is direct memory-tool guidance during compaction, not the backend contract itself; the strict validation command was run and reported `RESULT: PASSED` despite a read-only temp-file warning from the validator shell
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7336:58472:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8231:- Questions addressed: implicit versus explicit session creation, session maintenance state, stop-time summarization, compaction survival, trusted session identity, stale-session cleanup, Public hybrid retrieval overlap
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7337:58473:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8232:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne has no first-class session object; continuity is mostly prompt discipline plus collection scoping; compaction preserves tool awareness but not live continuity state; Public already has server-minted session IDs, working-memory continuity, cached summary validation, session-stop autosave, and stale cleanup; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` despite the shell warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7599:58955:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:46:- Questions addressed: dedup/upsert behavior, revision tracking, delete semantics, garbage collection, soft deletes, topic key stability, compaction hygiene guidance, auditability of mutation flows
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7600:58956:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:47:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper exposes no dedup, no upsert, no revision lineage, no soft-delete lifecycle, and no retention/GC contract; contradiction hygiene is primarily agent guidance plus hard delete; basename-derived project scoping is materially less stable than Public’s canonical-path plus logical-key approach; the compaction reminder block is the only clear hygiene improvement worth borrowing immediately; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7707:59245:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5111:- Questions addressed: agent-agnostic plugin shape, passive capture vs manual capture, multi-agent safety, project/global scoping ergonomics, AGENTS guidance, compaction survival, hybrid-search boundary, CocoIndex separation, offline/local integration assumptions.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7708:59246:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5112:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper is intentionally minimal and portable, but it delegates nearly all safe behavior to agent discipline; it has no passive capture or actor-scoped governance, its best transferable idea is the repeated memory-discipline checklist across AGENTS and compaction, and its project-first/global-lazy ergonomics are useful even though the underlying scope model is too weak for Public.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7725:59275:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:49:- Questions answered: the wrapper is thin; hybrid ranking is README-level not wrapper-owned; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval; project/global split is useful but collision-prone; compaction tool reminders are the best adopt-now idea; CocoIndex should stay separate from memory retrieval

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9358:131110:- Questions addressed: compaction-survival leverage, governed mutation mismatch, project/global ergonomics, AGENTS/startup reuse, `core=true` semantics, OpenCode plugin layering, offline-first tradeoffs, current hybrid-retrieval overlap, CocoIndex separation.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9359:131111:- Questions answered: Phase A adopt now: add a deduped memory-tool reminder to the existing compaction transport, and rewrite startup/AGENTS wording around `session_bootstrap` plus governed save flows. Phase B prototype later: add read-only `project/global` recall aliases that compile down to current retrieval scope, with no write aliases. Phase C hold or reject: do not add a direct `core=true` shortcut, do not replace Public’s retrieval stack with Mnemosyne, and only pursue an OpenCode-native facade if it delegates to MCP instead of becoming a second authority.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9360:131112:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s genuinely new value for Public is not hybrid ranking, it is wrapper ergonomics plus compaction-time memory awareness.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:38:- What I would do differently: for iteration 32, I would convert each Q1/Q2/Q3 milestone into packet-ready success criteria and explicit verification gates so the roadmap can be executed without re-arguing sequencing.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:84:## Assessment
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:85:- **New information ratio**: `0.23`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:86:- **Questions addressed**: unit-vs-integration boundaries, memory-quality regression strategy, compaction/bootstrapping verification, scope/offline-failure testing, and how much of Mnemosyne to test directly versus as an adapter contract.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:87:- **Questions answered**: wrapper behavior should be unit-tested with mocks; stateful Public behaviors should use real SQLite fixtures; retrieval quality should be gated by ablation/ground-truth metrics; compaction needs payload snapshots; scope/offline matrices should precede any facade rollout.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:88:- **Novelty justification**: This pass adds a concrete three-lane testing architecture tied to current Public sources instead of only comparing feature patterns at the design level.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:92:## Ruled Out
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:97:## Reflection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:99:- **What did not work**: The external repo offers no observable backend internals and minimal packaging metadata, so novel insight came from boundary analysis rather than deeper implementation inspection.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:100:- **What I would do differently**: In iteration 34, I would convert this into explicit CI lanes and named fixture packs with pass/fail thresholds per lane.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:102:## Recommended Next Focus

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:119:- Questions addressed: [list]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:120:- Questions answered: [list]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:125:- New information ratio: `0.24`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:126:- Questions addressed: `[search latency trade-offs, storage growth controls, indexing overhead, startup-time impact, compaction overhead, code-search separation]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:127:- Questions answered: `[Public’s main costs come from ranking depth and startup integrity work; its main savings come from incremental indexing and active-corpus reduction; Mnemosyne’s main advantage is cheap wrapper ergonomics, not lower total runtime cost]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:128:- Novelty justification: `This pass adds a performance-specific synthesis that separates thin plugin overhead from backend cold-start cost and maps Mnemosyne’s cheap compaction/scoping ergonomics onto Public’s heavier but more resilient retrieval lifecycle.`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:131:## Ruled Out
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:136:## Reflection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:138:- What did not work: CocoIndex semantic search was not usable in this turn, so I relied on targeted source reads and exact-term sweeps for the Public-side evidence.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:139:- What I would do differently: next pass should turn these static findings into explicit p50/p95 measurements for `memory_search`, startup scan, and `memory_index_scan` across small, medium, and large fixture corpora.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:141:## Recommended Next Focus

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:176:- What did not work: the external repo proves almost nothing about backend ranking internals, so the highest-confidence findings came from interface and lifecycle analysis, not deeper algorithm inspection.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-037.md:177:- What I would do differently: next pass should turn these conclusions into a concrete adapter matrix with three columns: transport-only prompt injection, MCP facade delegation, and explicit non-goals.

   158	};
   159	
   160	// ChunkReassemblyResult — now imported from lib/search/chunk-reassembly.ts
   161	
   162	type IntentWeights = IntentClassifierWeights;
   163	
   164	function toIntentWeightsConfig(weights: IntentWeights | null): IntentWeightsConfig | null {
   165	  if (!weights) return null;

   168	    importance: weights.importance,

   203	  includeArchived?: boolean; // REQ-206: include archived memories in search (default false)
   204	  enableSessionBoost?: boolean;

   212	  profile?: string;

   214	  retrievalLevel?: 'local' | 'global' | 'auto';
   215	}
   216	
   217	// resolveRowContextType — now imported from lib/search/search-utils.ts
   218	// resolveEvalScore, collectEvalChannelsFromRow — now imported from lib/telemetry/eval-channel-tracking.ts
   219	
   220	function attachTelemetryMeta(
   221	  response: MCPResponse,
   222	  telemetryPayload: Record<string, unknown>,
   223	): MCPResponse {
   224	  const firstEntry = response?.content?.[0];
   225	  if (!firstEntry || typeof firstEntry.text !== 'string') {
   226	    return response;
   227	  }
   228	
   229	  try {
   230	    const envelope = JSON.parse(firstEntry.text) as Record<string, unknown>;
   231	    const meta = envelope.meta && typeof envelope.meta === 'object'
   232	      ? envelope.meta as Record<string, unknown>
   233	      : {};
   234	    envelope.meta = {
   235	      ...meta,
   236	      _telemetry: telemetryPayload,
   237	    };
   238	
   239	    return {
   240	      ...response,
   241	      content: [{ ...firstEntry, text: JSON.stringify(envelope, null, 2) }],
   242	    };
   243	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   244	    const message = toErrorMessage(error);
   245	    console.warn('[memory-search] Failed to attach telemetry payload:', message);
   246	    return response;
   247	  }
   248	}
   249	
   250	function extractResponseResults(response: MCPResponse): Array<Record<string, unknown>> {
   251	  const firstEntry = response?.content?.[0];
   252	  if (!firstEntry || typeof firstEntry.text !== 'string') {
   253	    return [];
   254	  }
   255	
   256	  try {
   257	    const envelope = JSON.parse(firstEntry.text) as Record<string, unknown>;
   258	    const data = envelope.data && typeof envelope.data === 'object'
   259	      ? envelope.data as Record<string, unknown>
   260	      : null;
   261	    return Array.isArray(data?.results)
   262	      ? data.results as Array<Record<string, unknown>>
   263	      : [];
   264	  } catch {
   265	    return [];
   266	  }
   267	}
   268	
   269	function extractSearchCachePayload(response: MCPResponse): SearchCachePayload | null {
   270	  const firstEntry = response?.content?.[0];
   271	  if (!firstEntry || typeof firstEntry.text !== 'string') {
   272	    return null;
   273	  }
   274	
   275	  try {
   276	    const envelope = JSON.parse(firstEntry.text) as Record<string, unknown>;
   277	    const summary = typeof envelope.summary === 'string' ? envelope.summary : null;
   278	    const data = envelope.data && typeof envelope.data === 'object'
   279	      ? envelope.data as Record<string, unknown>
   280	      : null;

   310	    return null;
   311	  }
   312

   319	    readiness: 'ready',
   320	    preferredTool: 'code_graph_query',

     2	// ║ Spec Kit Compact Code Graph OpenCode Plugin                              ║
     3	// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
     4	'use strict';
     5	
     6	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     7	// 1. OVERVIEW
     8	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     9	// Thin OpenCode plugin hook layer for packet 030.
    10	// Important runtime boundary: never import the MCP server bundle directly
    11	// inside the OpenCode host process because its native modules (better-sqlite3,
    12	// sqlite-vec) may be compiled for a different Node ABI than the host runtime.
    13	// Instead, call a plain `node` bridge process that loads the server bundle in
    14	// the user's normal Node environment and returns the transport plan as JSON.
    15	
    16	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    17	// 2. IMPORTS

    35

    45	
    46	/**
    47	 * @typedef {{

    54	
    55	/**
